implementation_decision.fallback: generate Python protobuf/grpc artifacts from spec/upstream/a2a.proto if pinned SDK drifts or cannot satisfy a conformance row

sdk.role: pinned official SDK runtime for generated proto/types, clients, server routes, task stores; local safety wrappers remain in hermes_a2a

known_spec_notes: v1.0.0 is latest released version in published versioned spec at retrieval time.
Section 1.4 says spec/a2a.proto is single authoritative normative definition of all protocol data objects and request/response messages.

REST SubscribeToTask docs list POST /tasks/{id}:subscribe while normative proto HTTP annotation uses GET /tasks/{id=*}:subscribe; official SDK accepts both GET and POST. Implement support for both and classify GET/proto as canonical.

license.compatibility: PASS for local vendoring/codegen; no public release authorized by this plan

normative_roles.spec/upstream/a2a-sdk-pypi-1.0.0.json: official Python SDK candidate metadata; external dependency candidate, not normative over proto/spec
normative_roles.spec/upstream/a2a.proto: single authoritative normative definition of protocol data objects and request/response messages for v1.0.0
normative_roles.spec/upstream/adr-001-protojson-serializ: normative-adjacent serialization ADR cited by spec for ProtoJSON naming/enum behavior
normative_roles.spec/upstream/buf.*: build/codegen configuration used by upstream; input to reproducible generation checks
normative_roles.spec/upstream/specification.md: normative prose specification; Section 1.4 states proto is single authoritative normative data/request-response definition

provenance.git_tag_verified_by: git clone --branch v1.0.0 --depth 1 and exact tag checkout
provenance.signature_status: no tag signature verification evidence captured; treated as unsigned-risk with source archive/tag cross-check required for release
